This covers: proper error handling (exit codes, correlation IDs), token auth (Bearer tokens, per-agent roles), Quack protocol integration for concurrent access, health check endpoint, and a systemd unit file. Blocking Phase 2 and Phase 3.

These aren't bugs — they're features. The graph preserves disagreement, not averages. **Design decisions:** values/goals format (comma-separated vs JSON array), whether values/goals are mutable by the agent. **PM input needed:** should values/goals be visible to all agents or private?

What edge type for 'this source supports this finding'?

title: Métis integration — zettelkasten notes → OHM nodes, wikilinks → OHM edges
description: Integrate Métis (working memory agent) with OHM. Convert Métis zettelkasten notes to OHM nodes, wikilinks between notes to OHM edges. Must handle: note content → node content field, wikilink [[target]] → DERIVES_FROM or REFERENCES edge, tags → node labels, confidence from Métis assessment. **Design decisions needed:** sync direction (Métis→OHM only vs bidirectional), note-to-node mapping strategy (one note = one node vs one note = multiple nodes), wikilink edge type selection. **PM input needed:** should Métis notes be L3 (Knowledge) or L2 (Flow)? What confidence threshold for auto-promotion from private?
close_reason: Closed

title: ohm diff — what changed between two timestamps
description: Implement ohm diff command. Given two ISO timestamps, show what changed in the graph between them. Uses DuckLake time travel to compare states. Output: added nodes, removed nodes, added edges, removed edges, confidence changes, challenge additions. Support --layer filter, --agent filter, --format json. **Design decisions needed:** diff granularity (field-level vs row-level), change categorization (added/removed/modified), output format for large diffs (summary vs full). **PM input needed:** should diff include confidence changes or only structural changes? Max time range for diff?
close_reason: Closed

title: ohm snapshot — query graph state at historical timestamp
description: Implement ohm snapshot command. Given an ISO timestamp, query the graph state as it existed at that time. Uses DuckLake time travel (SELECT ... AS OF). Must support: snapshot <timestamp> (full graph state), snapshot <timestamp> --node <id> (single node history), snapshot <timestamp> --edge <id> (single edge history). Output: human-readable summary and --format json. **Design decisions needed:** timestamp resolution (second vs millisecond), timezone handling (UTC vs local), snapshot caching for repeated queries. **PM input needed:** should snapshots be exportable to file for offline analysis?
close_reason: Closed

title: Local DuckDB cache per agent — sync on heartbeat
description: Implement per-agent local DuckDB cache that syncs from DuckLake on heartbeat. Each agent (Métis, Clio, Hephaestus, Socrates) gets its own DuckDB file for working memory. Sync flow: heartbeat tick → check DuckLake table_changes() since last sync → apply incremental changes to local cache → push local changes to DuckLake → update last_sync timestamp. Must handle: initial full sync, incremental updates, conflict detection, offline mode (queue changes for later sync). **Design decisions needed:** cache file location (XDG data dir vs project dir), sync batch size, retry strategy for failed syncs. **PM input needed:** should agents share a single cache or have independent caches? Offline sync priority (immediate vs batched)?
close_reason: Closed

title: DuckLake integration — shared backend with time travel and change feed
description: Integrate DuckLake as the shared backend for canonical truth, time travel, and change feed. DuckLake provides: table_changes() for incremental sync, time travel queries (SELECT ... AS OF), data inlining for small payloads. Must support: local DuckDB cache per agent synced from DuckLake on heartbeat, incremental sync via table_changes() (not full dump), conflict resolution for concurrent writes. **Design decisions needed:** DuckLake format version pinning, sync heartbeat interval, conflict resolution strategy (last-write-wins vs merge vs agent-acknowledged). **PM input needed:** sync frequency (heartbeat interval), conflict resolution strategy, DuckLake storage location (local vs S3 vs network share).
close_reason: Closed

title: ohm state — set and query agent focus
description: Implement ohm state commands: ohm state 'researching X' (set current focus), ohm state show (my state), ohm state show <agent> (another agent's state), ohm state who-is-working-on <topic> (find collaborators), ohm state history (my focus history). State stored in ohm_agent_state table. **Design decisions needed:** focus history retention, state expiry (auto-clear after inactivity), privacy controls (can agents hide their state?). **PM input needed:** state TTL (how long before focus auto-clears?), should agents be able to set private state invisible to others?

title: ohm graph listen — change feed since last check
description: Implement ohm graph listen command. Shows changes since last check or explicit timestamp. Change feed format (from docs/cli.md): [L3] métis created CAUSES edge: and_or_pattern → hungary_democratic_escape (conf: 0.94). Must track per-agent last-check timestamp. Support --since (ISO timestamp or 'last-check'), --format json. **Design decisions needed:** change feed storage (DuckDB audit table vs DuckLake table_changes()), retention window for change history, deduplication of repeated reads. **PM input needed:** change feed retention period, should changes be grouped by agent or chronological?

title: Boundary enforcement — no agent overwrites another agent's edges
description: Implement the core boundary rules from ADR-003: (1) Any agent can write to L1 and L2 with attribution. (2) Only the owning agent can update L3/L4 edges. (3) Any agent can create CHALLENGED_BY/SUPPORTS/REFINES edges referencing any L3/L4 edge. (4) No agent can delete another agent's edge. (5) Private layer is never shared. (6) Promotion from private to shared is per-agent. Enforcement at both CLI layer (validation before Quack call) and DB layer (CHECK constraints or triggers). Exit code 4 for permission denied. **PM input needed:** should there be a 'super-admin' role that can override boundaries? What about agent decommissioning — who adopts orphaned edges?

title: ohm graph challenge/support — create challenge and support edges
description: Implement ohm graph challenge and ohm graph support commands. Challenge: create CHALLENGED_BY edge referencing target edge, with --reason and --confidence. Support: create SUPPORTS edge with --reason and --confidence. Must enforce boundary rules: cannot modify or delete target edge, only reference it. Validate: target edge exists, target edge is L3 or L4 (L1/L2 cannot be challenged), challenge_of field links back to original. **PM input needed:** should challenges be allowed on already-challenged edges (nested challenges)? Should an agent challenge its own edges?

title: ohm graph confidence — provenance and challenge audit
description: Implement ohm graph confidence command. Given an edge ID, show: original edge details (owner, confidence, provenance, created_at), all CHALLENGED_BY edges with reasons and confidences, all SUPPORTS edges with evidence, confidence history (if updated), challenge ratio (challenges / total references). Output: human-readable summary and --format json for agent consumption. **PM input needed:** what constitutes a 'confidence score' for an edge with multiple challenges? Average? Weighted? Show all?

title: ohm graph query — natural language and structured query
description: Implement ohm graph query command. Two modes: (1) structured query with filters (--type, --layer, --owner, --confidence-min, --created-after), (2) natural language query that maps to structured filters via keyword matching or LLM. Must support --format json for agent consumption. **Design decisions needed:** NL query parsing approach (regex patterns vs LLM call vs embedding search), query result ranking (by confidence? by recency?), query history persistence. **PM input needed:** should NL query use an LLM call (adds latency/cost) or keyword matching only? What query patterns are most important?

title: ohm graph neighborhood — bounded-depth traversal via CTE
description: Implement ohm graph neighborhood command. Wraps recursive CTE for bounded-depth graph traversal. Parameters: node-id (required), --depth (default 3, max configurable), --layer (filter by L1-L4), --direction (outgoing/incoming/both), --format (human/json). Output: list of edges with layer, type, confidence, owner. **Design decisions needed:** cycle detection in CTE (track visited nodes), performance guardrails at high depth, result pagination for large neighborhoods. **PM input needed:** default depth limit, max depth before warning, pagination page size.

title: ohm graph write — create nodes and edges with attribution
description: Implement ohm graph write command. Must support: --from (node ID), --to (node ID), --type (edge type), --layer (L1-L4), --confidence (float 0-1), --condition (optional context string), --provenance (source attribution). Auto-attach created_by from authenticated agent token. Validate: edge type matches layer constraints (L1: CONTAINS/BELONGS_TO/HAS_COMPONENT, L2: DERIVES_FROM/INFLUENCES/REFERENCES, L3: CAUSES/CORRELATES_WITH/PREDICTS/EXPLAINS, L4: EXPECTS/PLANS/RISKS/DEPENDS_ON), confidence range, node existence. **Design decisions needed:** auto-create nodes on first reference vs require pre-creation, batch write support, idempotency keys for retry safety. **PM input needed:** should nodes be auto-created on write or must they exist first?

title: Quack concurrent access test — multi-agent read/write validation
description: Write integration tests that validate multiple simulated agents can read and write the graph simultaneously through Quack. Test scenarios: concurrent writes to different nodes, concurrent reads during write, challenge edge creation during active write, token auth rejection for invalid tokens, role-based permission enforcement. **PM input needed:** target number of concurrent agents for load testing, acceptable latency thresholds.
notes: Scaffold exists in server.py but needs production hardening
close_reason: Closed

title: Recursive CTE views — parameterized graph traversal queries
description: Implement ~7 parameterized CTE views for common graph query patterns: neighborhood (bounded-depth traversal), path (shortest path between nodes), impact (downstream failure analysis), confidence audit (edge + all challenges/supports), change feed (timestamp-based diff), agent state (current focus per agent), stats (counts by layer/type). Must work through Quack. **Design decisions needed:** CTE parameterization strategy (SQL functions vs application-layer query building), depth limits and performance guardrails, result set size limits. **PM input needed:** default depth limits per query type, timeout thresholds for long-running traversals.

title: Quack protocol integration — concurrent multi-agent access
description: Integrate DuckDB's Quack HTTP protocol for multi-reader/multi-writer concurrent access. Requires: Quack server startup in ohmd, token-based authentication per agent, role-based access control (admin/read-write/read-only), connection pooling. Must pin Quack binary from core_nightly for production stability. **Design decisions needed:** Quack version pinning strategy, token storage (env vars vs config file vs secrets manager), rate limiting per agent. **PM input needed:** agent role hierarchy, token issuance workflow, max concurrent connections per agent.
notes: Scaffold exists in server.py but needs production hardening
close_reason: Closed

title: ohm CLI scaffold — command tree and argument parsing
description: Scaffold the ohm CLI with the command tree defined in docs/cli.md. Use click (Python) or argparse. Commands: ohm serve {start,stop,status,config}, ohm graph {schema,layers,status,query,neighborhood,write,challenge,support,listen,confidence,impact,path,stats}, ohm state {show,who-is-working-on,history}, ohm snapshot, ohm diff. Support --format json flag on all commands. **Design decisions needed:** CLI framework choice (click vs typer vs argparse), output formatting library (rich vs tabulate), config file discovery (XDG vs cwd vs explicit path). **PM input needed:** preferred CLI framework, tab-completion requirements (bash/zsh/fish/powershell), telemetry opt-in.

title: OHM database schema — nodes, edges, observations, agent_state
description: Implement the SQL schema defined in docs/schema.md. Tables: ohm_nodes (id, label, type, content, created_by, visibility, provenance, confidence), ohm_edges (from_node, to_node, layer, edge_type, confidence, condition, challenge_of, challenge_type), ohm_observations (node_id, edge_id, type, value, baseline, sigma, source), ohm_agent_state (agent_name, current_focus, active_patterns, last_sync, confidence_threshold, available_services). **Design decisions needed:** index strategy for CTE traversal queries, partitioning strategy (by layer? by time?), migration framework for schema evolution. **PM input needed:** data retention policy, archival strategy for old observations.

title: ohmd daemon scaffold — persistent DuckDB process
description: Design and implement the ohmd daemon that owns the DuckDB file and runs the Quack server. Must support: systemd unit file, health check endpoint, token auth with role-based access per agent, graceful shutdown with checkpoint. **Design decisions needed:** config file format (TOML/YAML/env), log rotation strategy, startup sequence (init DB → start Quack → health check → accept connections). **PM input needed:** deployment topology (single host vs container), systemd vs Docker, log retention policy, token rotation cadence.
notes: Scaffold exists in server.py but needs production hardening
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 3: Agent Integration — Each Olympus agent uses OHM as its knowledge graph
description: Goal: All agents reading/writing via OHM. Kuzu deprecated. Week 7-8.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 2: Change Feed + Awareness — Agents see what other agents are thinking
description: Goal: Change feed works. Agent state visible. Local caches sync from DuckLake. Week 5-6.
notes: Phase 2: Change feed + awareness. listen and state are done (OHM-5zg.1, OHM-5zg.2). Remaining: DuckLake integration (OHM-5zg.3), local cache sync (OHM-5zg.4), snapshot (OHM-5zg.5), diff (OHM-5zg.6).
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 1: Core Operations — Read and write the graph via CLI
description: Goal: Agents can create, read, and challenge edges. Boundary rules enforced. Week 3-4.

title: Phase 0: Foundation — ohmd daemon + Quack server + basic schema
description: Goal: ohmd daemon runs, Quack serves connections, schema exists, CLI scaffold works. Multiple agents can connect concurrently. Week 1-2.

title: Bayesian inference must consume outcomes and probability observations
description: Child of OHM-vatf. The Bayesian engine builds networks from raw ohm_edges.confidence and ignores (1) ohm_outcomes records that falsify upstream claims, and (2) ohm_observations with scale=probability on target/non-root nodes. Reproduced on hormuz_and_gate July 3, 2026: 7 falsified outcomes lowered path confidences (Abraham Accords 0.94->0.49) but /inference posterior remained P(bad)=0.9584. Required: (a) record_outcome should modulate effective edge probability/confidence in inference, (b) probability-scaled observations should be converted to virtual/soft evidence, (c) support/challenge observations must not be stored as probability priors, (d) temporal decay should reduce weight of stale observations, (e) evidence param should accept both = and : separators. Blocks agentic causal inference and AND->OR model learning.
notes:
Adversarial test spec for evidence consumption (metis 2026-07-03):
1. Create a test DAG: root_claim CAUSES target (confidence 0.9). Run inference: P(target=bad) should be high.
2. Record outcome=False on root_claim. Re-run inference: P(target=bad) should decrease measurably.
3. Add observation scale='probability' value=0.1 on target with no parents. Re-run inference: posterior should approach 0.10.
4. Add two probability observations: value=0.9 (30 days old) and value=0.1 (today). Run inference with half_life_days=7. Posterior should be closer to 0.1 than 0.9.
5. Verify ?evidence=node_id:state and ?evidence=node_id=state both parse and return different posteriors.
6. Verify support/challenge observations do not get stored with scale='probability' unless explicitly marked.
Expected: inference consumes outcomes as edge-weight updates and probability observations as soft/virtual evidence.
Patch proposals drafted by metis 2026-07-03:
- /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_bayesian_observation_soft_evidence.patch
- /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_bayesian_outcome_edge_weights.patch
These are design sketches, not tested merges. They need code review and unit tests before deployment.
metis 2026-07-03: Deeper finding — observations lack semantic roles. The schema conflates obs_type (measurement/support/challenge) with scale (probability/count/etc). Old support/challenge observations with scale='probability' are being averaged as root priors, causing permanently inflated P(bad) on hormuz_and_gate. Proposed fix: introduce ObservationSemanticRoles class mapping (obs_type, scale) to inference behavior. Draft ADR and patches at /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_observation_semantic_roles*.
metis 2026-07-03: Empirical proof-of-concept validates the design. Script: /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_bayesian_poc.py. Findings:
- Synthetic 5-root DAG with P(bad)=0.9 roots produces P(target=bad)=0.9994 (matches Hormuz ~0.96).
- Hard evidence on target fails with pgmpy error.
- Virtual evidence P(bad)=0.18 alone only drops to 0.9972 (swamped by strong roots).
- Outcome-derived root-prior reduction (4 roots -> P=0.1) drops to 0.86.
- Both together drop to 0.5742.
Conclusion: both patches are required. Virtual evidence alone is insufficient; outcome records must also reduce root/edge weights.
metis 2026-07-03: Delivered complete artifact set:
1. Design ADR: /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_observation_semantic_roles_design.md
2. ObservationSemanticRoles patch: /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_observation_semantic_roles.patch
3. Soft evidence patch: /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_bayesian_observation_soft_evidence.patch
4. Outcome edge-weight patch: /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_bayesian_outcome_edge_weights.patch
5. Combined fix patch: /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_bayesian_combined_fix.patch
6. Empirical PoC: /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_bayesian_poc.py
7. Data migration SQL: /root/olympus/metis/patches/20260703_OHM_migrate_misscaled_observations.sql

Root cause chain confirmed:
- support/challenge observations were stored with scale='probability'
- build_bayesian_network averages them as root priors
- pgmpy rejects hard evidence on target
- record_outcome never touches CPDs
- Result: hormuz_and_gate P(bad) stuck at 0.9584 despite resolved Iran-internal gate.
HANDOFF PACKAGE — metis 2026-07-03 20:51

What we know:
- Reproduced on hormuz_and_gate: /inference returns P(bad)=0.9584 even after (a) 7 falsified outcomes on upstream root nodes, (b) a reconciled P(fail)=0.18 observation.
- Root cause is 4-fold:
  1. support/challenge observations were stored with scale='probability' and build_bayesian_network() averages them as root-node priors (ignores obs_type).
  2. pgmpy rejects hard evidence on the query variable, so probability observations on the target cannot be used as hard evidence.
  3. record_outcome() writes to ohm_outcomes but build_bayesian_network() reads raw ohm_edges.confidence; outcomes never affect CPDs.
  4. No half-life decay by default (half_life_days=0.0), so stale priors dominate forever.

Required fixes:
- Introduce ObservationSemanticRoles mapping (obs_type, scale) to inference behavior.
- Convert probability-scaled measurement observations to virtual/soft evidence.
- Modulate edge confidence by source reliability and falsified-claim status.
- Default temporal decay to non-zero for dynamic domains.
- Accept '=' in evidence query parameter.

Artifacts (all under /root/olympus/metis/patches/):
- 20260703_OHM_observation_semantic_roles_design.md (ADR)
- 20260703_OHM_observation_semantic_roles.patch
- 20260703_OHM_bayesian_observation_soft_evidence.patch
- 20260703_OHM_bayesian_outcome_edge_weights.patch
- 20260703_OHM_bayesian_combined_fix.patch
- 20260703_OHM_bayesian_poc.py (empirical proof of interdependence)
- 20260703_OHM_migrate_misscaled_observations.sql

Empirical result (PoC):
- 5 stale roots at P=0.9 -> P(target=bad)=0.9994
- virtual evidence alone -> 0.9972 (not enough)
- outcome reduction alone -> 0.8600 (not enough)
- both together -> 0.5742 (calibrated)

Recommended owner: Hephaestus (already owns task-ohm-semanticroles-bayesian-enables-influences-2026-06-27, which is the companion edge-type semantic fix).

Next steps for dev team:
1. Review combined patch and decide on module placement.
2. Add unit tests using 20260703_OHM_bayesian_poc.py as template.
3. Run regression tests against live graph snapshot.
4. Decide migration policy for existing mis-scaled observations.
5. Coordinate with SemanticRoles.bayesian task so INFLUENCES/ENABLES and observation semantics land together.
metis 2026-07-04: Built a self-contained regression test that reproduces and validates the fix using a live snapshot of ohm.duckdb.

New artifacts (all under /root/olympus/metis/patches/):
- 20260704_OHM_bayesian_minimal_fix.patch — clean three-part fix that applies with patch -p3 in src/ohm/inference:
  1. Lower outcome falsification threshold from >=2 to >0 false outcomes.
  2. Filter root priors to measurement/experiment_result observations only.
  3. Convert target probability observations to virtual/soft evidence.
- 20260704_OHM_bayesian_regression_test.py — self-contained pytest-style script:
  - Copies OHM src to a temp dir
  - Applies the minimal fix
  - Runs original vs patched inference against ohm_snapshot.duckdb
  - Asserts P(bad) drops significantly
- ohm_snapshot.duckdb + ohm_snapshot.wal — read-only snapshot of live OHM taken 2026-07-04 08:33 EDT.

Test result (2026-07-04):
- Original bayesian.py: P(hormuz_and_gate=bad) = 0.9485
- Patched bayesian.py:   P(hormuz_and_gate=bad) = 0.4575
- Delta: -0.4910

Key findings from the live snapshot:
- 11 falsified outcomes exist on Hormuz-related claim nodes (e.g., hormuz_and_gate itself, abraham_accords_sixth_and_gate_layer..., event-irgc-formal-hormuz-closure-day106, etc.).
- The current code requires >=2 outcomes per node before applying an outcome discount, so most of these single false outcomes are ignored. Lowering the threshold is the dominant fix.
- Target virtual evidence helps calibrate the posterior once the strong root priors are discounted.
- The default max_nodes=50 truncates aggressively; with the fix it still works, but larger values (150-200) stabilize around P(bad)=0.73.

Next steps for dev team:
1. Review 20260704_OHM_bayesian_minimal_fix.patch and decide on the outcome threshold policy.
2. Add proper unit tests in OHM/tests/ using the regression test as a template.
3. Consider exposing max_nodes as an inference parameter or tuning the truncation heuristic for nodes with falsified outcomes.
4. Run the existing OHM test suite after applying the patch.

metis 2026-07-04: Patched the patch and ran the existing OHM Bayesian test suite against it in an isolated temp copy of src/ohm.

Result:
- 131 passed, 1 failed
- The single failure is tests/test_bayesian.py::TestOutcomeBasedAdjustment::test_single_outcome_no_discount, which explicitly asserts that a single falsified outcome should NOT trigger a discount. The minimal fix changes that threshold from >=2 to >0, so this test must be updated if the new policy is adopted.
- The earlier UnboundLocalError was caused by an inner `from pgmpy.factors.discrete import TabularCPD` inside the new target-observation loop; removed it and now the patch reuses the module-level import.

Updated artifacts:
- 20260704_OHM_bayesian_minimal_fix.patch now applies cleanly and avoids the inner-import bug.
- 20260704_OHM_bayesian_regression_test.py updated accordingly.

Implication: the patch is code-correct. The only remaining question is whether the dev team wants to accept single-outcome falsifications as sufficient evidence to discount an edge. The live Hormuz data strongly supports this — 9 of 11 falsified outcomes are single-record and currently ignored.

metis 2026-07-04: Refined the minimal fix after testing target-node virtual evidence.

What changed:
- Removed the target-virtual-evidence change from the minimal fix. It produced unstable results (e.g., oil_or_gate_pricing P(bad) jumped to 0.9930) because the live observations include non-probability values like Brent prices (70.34) and the default half_life_days=0 gives equal weight to old and noisy observations.
- Kept the two-part fix:
  1. Lower outcome falsification threshold from >=2 to >0.
  2. Filter root priors to measurement/experiment_result observations only.

Updated results across real targets:
- hormuz_and_gate: 0.9485 -> 0.7935 (-0.1550)
- concept-warsh-rate-hike-collision: 0.4171 -> 0.2097 (-0.2074)
- concept-dual-chokepoint-lock-day103: 0.4171 -> 0.2097 (-0.2074)
- oil_or_gate_pricing: 0.6457 -> 0.5897 (-0.0560)
- trump_approval_and_gate_layer_11: 0.5720 -> 0.4240 (-0.1480)

Test suite:
- 131 passed, 1 failed (test_single_outcome_no_discount — expected because it asserts the old threshold policy).

Updated artifacts:
- 20260704_OHM_bayesian_minimal_fix.patch — now two-part, applies cleanly.
- 20260704_OHM_bayesian_regression_test.py — updated assertions.
- 20260704_OHM_bayesian_extended_examples.py — matrix across five real targets plus anticipated failure modes.

Anticipated cases / follow-ups:
1. Target virtual evidence needs a proper half-life/recency policy and non-probability value filtering before it can be safely enabled.
2. max_nodes=50 truncation can drop falsified nodes; expose max_nodes or privilege outcome-bearing nodes in truncation.
3. Source reliability modulation still not applied to edge weights.
4. INFLUENCES semantics contradiction remains filed as OHM-mzyc.1.

title: task nodes accept invalid task_status and unknown assigned_to
description: POST /tasks (or task node creation) accepts arbitrary task_status values like invalid_status, closed, cancelled and stores them verbatim. It also accepts unknown assigned_to values like not-an-agent and clio-vs-agent_clio fragmentation. This breaks the documented task lifecycle and fragments agent queues.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Reject or normalize unknown task_status values to the canonical lifecycle (open, in_progress, review, done, blocked, cancelled). 2) Reject or map unknown assigned_to to a known OHM agent, or return a warning nudge. 3) Add validation tests.
close_reason: Shipped by Hephaestus 2026-07-02.

title: sync-beads is not idempotent and dry_run crashes
description: POST /admin/sync-beads rewrites the same 9 tasks on every call even when nothing changed in Beads. This generates unnecessary write churn and may invalidate caches/embeddings/change listeners. Additionally, dry_run=true returns a 500 duplicate-key error on 'beads-backlog-anchor' instead of simulating the sync. Filter parameters (assignee, state, since) appear to be silently ignored.
acceptance_criteria: 1) sync-beads is idempotent: unchanged issues do not update OHM task nodes. 2) dry_run=true returns a simulated result without writing. 3) assignee/state/since filters are enforced or rejected with 400 for invalid values. 4) Add tests for idempotency and dry_run.
close_reason: Shipped by Hephaestus 2026-07-02.

title: INFLUENCES edge type has contradictory causal status
description:
During testing of the new edge-typing guardrails (OHM-ezt5/tsxk/bm5r), we found that INFLUENCES is treated inconsistently:

- /edge/suggest-type never returns INFLUENCES and never marks it participates_in_inference=true.
- /schema lists INFLUENCES only under L2 edge types (no metadata object).
- /inference does not include INFLUENCES edges in the Bayesian network (Target node ... not in Bayesian network).
- However, the POST /edge nudge for INFLUENCES says: 'INFLUENCES edge feeds the Bayesian network. This edge will be used by /inference, /intervene, /sensitivity, /voi, and /game.'

This is a contradiction that will confuse agents. Either INFLUENCES is causal (and should participate in inference) or it is not (and the nudge text is wrong).
acceptance_criteria: Decide whether INFLUENCES is causal in OHM's Bayesian network. If causal, add it to EDGE_CONSTRAINTS with participates_in_inference=true, include it in inference builder, and ensure suggest-type can return it. If non-causal, change the nudge from causal_edge_confirmed to non_causal_edge_confirmed and update /schema metadata. Add an end-to-end test.
close_reason: Shipped by Hephaestus 2026-07-02.

title: OHM: agent edge-typing guardrails (epic)
description: Epic to reduce the cost of correcting edge-type mistakes in OHM. Agents repeatedly type pattern-to-case relationships as causal edges, which pollutes Bayesian inference and requires expensive challenges. The deliverables are: an advisory suggest-type endpoint, creation-time nudges, a mechanism gate for causal edges, and a bug fix for SDK challenge_edge.

title: OHM: SDK challenge_edge() creates malformed CHALLENGED_BY edge
description: Fix the HTTP SDK `challenge_edge()` method so it creates a proper `CHALLENGED_BY` edge between the source concept and the target node. Currently it appears to produce an edge whose `from_node` is set to the literal string "CAUSES" instead of the intended source node, making the challenge unsearchable and unusable for inference or confidence auditing.
design:
## Observed behavior
During the Deepthought drill on 2026-07-02, calling `HttpGraph.challenge_edge(edge_id="09313723-9a2c-4d5c-8336-d76ec760b9e9", reason=..., confidence=0.75, challenge_type="CHALLENGED_BY")` returned an edge record with:

```json
{
  "from_node": "CAUSES",
  "to_node": "concept-trump-binary-choice-deal-or-strikes",
  "edge_type": "CHALLENGED_BY",
  "challenge_of": "09313723-9a2c-4d5c-8336-d76ec760b9e9"
}
```

The source node should have been `aramco_ceo_2027_irreversibility_threshold` (or another concept chosen by the agent). Instead, the original edge's `edge_type` string was used as the `from_node`.

## Expected behavior
`challenge_edge()` should create a `CHALLENGED_BY` edge where:
- `from_node` is a node chosen by the challenging agent (or a default challenge concept node)
- `to_node` is the original edge's target (or the original edge itself, depending on schema)
- `challenge_of` points to the original edge ID
- The edge is findable via `search_edges()` and `/neighborhood` of the source concept

## Likely cause
The server-side `challenge_edge()` store method probably constructs the challenge edge by reading the original edge's fields and assigning them positionally. If the signature expects `(from_node, to_node, edge_type, ...)` but the call passes `(edge_id, reason, confidence, challenge_type, agent)`, or if the original edge's `edge_type` is accidentally copied into `from_node`, the result is the observed bug.

## Reproduction
```python
from ohm.sdk import connect_http
g = connect_http("http://127.0.0.1:8710", actor="metis", token=...)
result = g.challenge_edge(
    edge_id="09313723-9a2c-4d5c-8336-d76ec760b9e9",
    reason="test challenge",
    confidence=0.75,
    challenge_type="CHALLENGED_BY"
)
print(result["from_node"])  # Expected: a valid node id; Actual: "CAUSES"
```

## Proposed fix
1. Inspect `src/ohm/store.py` (or equivalent) `challenge_edge()` method.
2. Ensure it fetches the original edge, then creates the new edge with `from_node` set to a real node. If the SDK does not specify `from_node`, default to a challenge-source node owned by the agent (e.g., `agent-{actor}-challenges`) or require the SDK to pass `from_node`.
3. Update `HttpGraph.challenge_edge()` signature to accept `from_node: str | None = None` and pass it to the server.
4. Add server validation that `from_node` exists and is not equal to an edge type string.
5. Update the direct SDK `challenge()` method (which uses `create_challenge`) to ensure it also creates valid challenge edges.

## Files to touch
- `src/ohm/store.py` or wherever `challenge_edge()` is implemented.
- `src/ohm/framework/sdk.py` — HTTP `challenge_edge()` signature.
- `src/ohm/queries.py` — `create_challenge()` if used by direct SDK.
- `src/ohm/server/handlers/graph.py` — `_post_challenge` route.
- Tests: add a regression test that asserts `from_node` is a valid node id after `challenge_edge()`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) HttpGraph.challenge_edge() accepts optional from_node and passes it to server. 2) Server validates from_node is a real node ID and rejects edge type strings. 3) Created CHALLENGED_BY edge has valid from_node, to_node, and challenge_of. 4) Edge is findable via search_edges() and appears in /neighborhood of from_node. 5) Regression test reproduces original 'CAUSES' bug and confirms fix. 6) Direct SDK challenge() method also produces valid edges.
notes:
Reproduced during Deepthought drill on 2026-07-02. Edge ID 09313723-9a2c-4d5c-8336-d76ec760b9e9. Workaround: use POST /batch with from/to/type manually specified.
2026-07-02 (metis): Confirmed fixed by OHM-7el6 (commit 6cd9dd6). The local ohmd was running pre-7el6 code. After merging origin/main and restarting ohmd (with migration 0.41.0/0.43.0 ordering fix), HTTP challenge_edge() now creates CHALLENGED_BY edges with correct from_node/to_node matching the original edge. Tested live on http://127.0.0.1:8710. Closing as duplicate/fixed.

title: OHM: mechanism gate for causal edges
description: Require or strongly nudge agents to provide a mediating mechanism when creating causal edges. This addresses the 2026-07-02 drill finding that `INFLUENCES` edges were created from forward-looking market narratives (e.g., Aramco CEO 2027 threshold → oil pricing model) without a stated causal pathway.
design:
## Problem
OHM already has a `causal_edge_missing_mechanism` nudge when no `condition` or `metadata.mechanism` is provided. It is a suggestion, not a gate. The 2026-07-02 drills showed that weak `INFLUENCES` edges from narrative quotes (Aramco CEO 2027 irreversibility threshold → oil OR-gate pricing) were created without mechanism and later challenged. The nudge alone did not prevent the bad edge.

## Proposal
Make the mechanism gate stronger for causal edges, without making it impossible to create a causal edge without a mechanism.

### Tiered gate
1. **If mechanism is provided** (`condition` or `metadata.mechanism` non-empty): allow normal confidence range (0–1), subject to source tier ceiling.
2. **If mechanism is missing and confidence ≥ 0.7**: emit a `warning` nudge and apply a soft confidence ceiling of 0.6 for future calibration. Do not block the write.
3. **If mechanism is missing and confidence ≥ 0.85**: emit an `error`-severity nudge and require the agent to set `metadata.mechanism` or `condition` before the edge is accepted. This can be bypassed with a new `force: true` field, recorded for provenance.

### Configuration
Add server config key `enforce_mechanism_gate` with values:
- `off` — current behavior (suggestion only)
- `soft` — warning + confidence ceiling (default)
- `hard` — error unless mechanism or `force: true`

### Mechanism quality check (future)
Store-side, do not block. But log mechanism presence in `ohm_nudge_log` and expose per-agent `causal_edges_without_mechanism_rate` in `/agent_state`.

### SDK impact
- Add `HttpGraph.create_edge(..., mechanism=None)` convenience argument that writes to `condition`.
- Add `HttpGraph.causal_link(cause, effect, mechanism, confidence, **kwargs)` that requires mechanism and refuses if `mechanism` is empty.

## Files to touch
- `src/ohm/server/nudges.py` — upgrade `causal_edge_missing_mechanism` nudge, add severity/confidence logic.
- `src/ohm/server/handlers/graph.py` — respect `enforce_mechanism_gate` config, support `force` override.
- `src/ohm/config.py` or `src/ohm/server/server.py` — add config key.
- `src/ohm/framework/sdk.py` — add `mechanism` convenience argument and `causal_link()` helper.
- Tests: `tests/test_nudges.py`, `tests/server/test_graph_handlers.py`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) POST /edge with CAUSES/DEPENDS_ON/BLOCKS/ENABLES/THREATENS and no mechanism returns causal_edge_missing_mechanism nudge with severity based on confidence and enforce_mechanism_gate config. 2) enforce_mechanism_gate=hard rejects write unless mechanism is present or force:true is set. 3) force:true accepted and recorded in metadata. 4) SDK create_edge() accepts mechanism= argument; causal_link() helper requires mechanism. 5) /agent_state exposes causal_edges_without_mechanism_rate. 6) Tests cover all three gate modes and force override.
notes:
Triggered by Deepthought drill on 2026-07-02 where aramco_ceo_2027_irreversibility_threshold INFLUENCES oil_or_gate_pricing was challenged for lacking a mechanism.
2026-07-02 (metis): No longer depends on OHM-ezt5 for correctness; can proceed independently. Shared suggest_edge_type helper is still the preferred implementation path.
2026-07-02 (metis): Implemented in commit c1dbe96 with OHM-tsxk. Mechanism-gate nudge in src/ohm/server/nudges.py warns when CAUSES/INFLUENCES/DEPENDS_ON edges are created without condition= or metadata.mechanism. Needs adversarial test harness (OHM-9zae) and can be closed after that.
close_reason: Shipped by Hephaestus 2026-07-02.

title: OHM: creation-time nudge for pattern-to-case causal edges
description: Add creation-time nudges that warn when a pattern/concept node is linked to an event/decision/case with a causal edge type. This complements the `/edge/suggest-type` endpoint by surfacing the same guidance automatically at write time.
design:
## Problem
OHM's nudge system already warns when a `source` node is used as the source or target of a causal edge. It does not warn when a `pattern` or `concept` node is linked to an `event`, `decision`, or `case` with a causal edge type. The 2026-07-02 drills showed multiple instances of `hormuz_and_gate CAUSES decision-energy-reentry-20260702` and `and_gate_migration CAUSES concept-trump-binary-choice-deal-or-strikes` that were later downgraded to `APPLIES_TO` or `INFLUENCES`.

## Proposal
Extend `generate_nudges()` in `src/ohm/server/nudges.py` to detect pattern-to-case causal typing and emit a `semantic_edge_warning` nudge at edge-creation time, similar to the existing source-node rule.

### Detection rule
Fire when:
- `edge_type` is in `CAUSAL_EDGE_TYPES` (`CAUSES`, `DEPENDS_ON`, `BLOCKS`, `ENABLES`, `INFLUENCES`, `THREATENS`)
- AND (`from_node` type is in {`pattern`, `concept`} AND `to_node` type is in {`event`, `decision`, `case`, `concept`})
- AND either the from node has tags like `and-or`, `pattern`, `structural`, or the provenance/notes mention keywords like "instance of", "applies", "pattern", "structural", "manifests"

### Nudge format
```json
{
  "type": "semantic_edge_warning",
  "severity": "warning",
  "message": "from_node 'hormuz_and_gate' is a pattern/concept and to_node 'decision-energy-reentry-20260702' is a decision. CAUSES will flow through Bayesian inference. If you mean this is an instance of the pattern, use APPLIES_TO or EXPLAINS instead.",
  "data": {
    "edge_type": "CAUSES",
    "from_node_type": "pattern",
    "to_node_type": "decision",
    "suggested_types": ["APPLIES_TO", "EXPLAINS", "INFLUENCES"],
    "participates_in_inference": true
  }
}
```

### Confidence soft ceiling
If the agent ignores the nudge and still creates the edge, do not block. But if no `condition` or `metadata.mechanism` is provided, lower the effective confidence ceiling for that edge in reliability calibration (tracked separately in a future issue for causal downgrade rate).

## Relation to /edge/suggest-type
This issue is the proactive nudge; `/edge/suggest-type` is the explicit query. Both should use the same `suggest_edge_type()` helper in `nudges.py` so behavior is consistent.

## Files to touch
- `src/ohm/server/nudges.py` — add pattern-to-case detection and nudge generation.
- `src/ohm/server/handlers/graph.py` — pass node types into `generate_nudges()` for edge action.
- Tests: `tests/test_nudges.py`, `tests/server/test_graph_handlers.py`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) pattern/concept -> event/decision/case edge with CAUSES returns semantic_edge_warning nudge suggesting APPLIES_TO/EXPLAINS/INFLUENCES. 2) Nudge includes participates_in_inference and explains why the type matters. 3) Detection considers node types, tags, and provenance keywords. 4) Write still succeeds if agent overrides. 5) New tests cover nudge in graph handler and generate_nudges().
notes:
Triggered by Atlas/Deepthought/Ledger OHM drills on 2026-07-02. Related OHM concept node: ohm_edge_type_nudges_for_naive_agents. Should share helper logic with OHM-ezt5 /edge/suggest-type endpoint.
2026-07-02 (metis): Implemented in commit c1dbe96 with OHM-bm5r. Creation-time nudges in src/ohm/server/nudges.py now detect pattern/concept/idea → decision/task/event/case CAUSES edges and warn with suggested REFINES/EXPLAINS. Needs adversarial test harness (OHM-9zae) and can be closed after that.
close_reason: Shipped by Hephaestus 2026-07-02.

title: OHM: /edge/suggest-type endpoint and participates_in_inference flag
description: Add an advisory endpoint that recommends the most semantically appropriate edge type for a proposed link, and expose whether any edge participates in Bayesian inference. This addresses the repeated pattern observed in 2026-07-02 drills where agents typed pattern-to-case AND-gate relationships as CAUSES and later had to challenge/downgrade them to APPLIES_TO/INFLUENCES.
design:
## Problem
During the 2026-07-02 agent drills, Atlas/Deepthought/Ledger repeatedly found that pattern-to-case AND-gate relationships had been typed as `CAUSES` and later challenged/downgraded to `APPLIES_TO` or `INFLUENCES`. This is expensive: each correction creates a new edge, a challenge record, and leaves Bayesian inference polluted until fixed.

Naive agents default to causal language because (a) "X causes Y" is the most natural way to say "X matters for Y", (b) OHM accepts `CAUSES` without asking whether a mechanism is claimed, and (c) agents cannot see which edges flow through the Bayesian network and which do not.

## Proposal
Add an advisory endpoint and an inference-participation flag so agents can choose the right type before writing.

### Endpoint
`GET /edge/suggest-type?from=X&to=Y&edge_type=CAUSES&notes=...`

Response:
```json
{
  "from_node_type": "pattern",
  "to_node_type": "decision",
  "requested_type": "CAUSES",
  "participates_in_inference": true,
  "suggested_types": [
    {"type": "APPLIES_TO", "reason": "from_node is a pattern and to_node is a concrete case; this is likely a structural application, not a mechanism", "participates_in_inference": false},
    {"type": "EXPLAINS", "reason": "pattern explains the case as an instance of a recurring structure", "participates_in_inference": false},
    {"type": "INFLUENCES", "reason": "if you believe the pattern weakly shapes the case through sentiment or framing", "participates_in_inference": true}
  ],
  "nudge": "CAUSES will move probabilities in /inference. Use APPLIES_TO unless you can state the causal pathway."
}
```

### Inference visibility
Every `POST /edge` and `POST /batch` response should include `participates_in_inference: true/false` based on the edge type's membership in `CAUSAL_EDGE_TYPES`.

## Rules for suggest-type
1. If either endpoint is type `source`, prefer `SUPPORTS`/`REFERENCES` over causal types.
2. If `from_node` is `pattern`/`concept` and `to_node` is `event`/`decision`/`case`, prefer `APPLIES_TO`/`EXPLAINS`/`MANIFESTS_AS` over `CAUSES`.
3. If notes contain keywords like "instance of", "pattern", "structural", "applies to", "manifests as", boost non-causal suggestions.
4. If `CAUSES`/`DEPENDS_ON`/`BLOCKS`/`ENABLES`/`THREATENS` is requested, set `participates_in_inference: true` and list enabled endpoints (`/inference`, `/intervene`, `/game`, `/sensitivity`, `/voi`).
5. If `INFLUENCES` is requested, note that it is weak causal and often misused for structural effects.

## SDK impact
- Add `HttpGraph.suggest_edge_type(from_node, to_node, edge_type=None, notes=None)`.
- Update `create_edge()` docstring and return value to highlight `participates_in_inference`.

## Files to touch
- `src/ohm/server/handlers/graph.py` — add `_get_edge_suggest_type` route.
- `src/ohm/server/nudges.py` — implement `suggest_edge_type()`.
- `src/ohm/server/server.py` — route registration.
- `src/ohm/framework/sdk.py` — HTTP SDK method.
- `src/ohm/sdk.py` — direct SDK method if any.
- Tests: `tests/server/test_graph_handlers.py`, `tests/test_nudges.py`.
acceptance_criteria: 1) GET /edge/suggest-type returns ranked suggestions with participates_in_inference. 2) POST /edge and POST /batch responses include participates_in_inference boolean. 3) SDK exposes suggest_edge_type() and documents participates_in_inference. 4) New tests cover pattern-to-case, source-to-claim, and causal suggestions. 5) No regressions in existing graph handler or nudge tests.
notes:
Triggered by Atlas/Deepthought/Ledger OHM drills on 2026-07-02. Related OHM concept node: ohm_edge_type_nudges_for_naive_agents. Zettelkasten note: /root/olympus/metis/zettelkasten/notes/20260702_OHM_Edge_Type_Nudges_for_Naive_Agents.md. Parent design theme: naive agents need soft nudges at edge-creation time.

2026-07-02 (metis): Implemented in commit a729eb1. GET /edge/suggest-type?from=<id>&to=<id> returns suggested_edge_type, suggested_layer, participates_in_inference, alternatives, reasoning. Used by creation nudges. Needs adversarial test harness (OHM-9zae) and can be closed after that.

title: TOPO: append-only observation assessment + follow-up state machine
description: P1 gap from TOPO migration analysis. TOPO's ObservationService implements immutable facts + append-only assessment revisions with allowed transitions, follow-up action state machine (open → in_progress → completed), and regime supersession (only one active regime observation per node). OHM has supersede_observation() and chain queries but no typed assessment lifecycle or follow-up action items. Needed before TOPO can migrate observation assessment logic to OHM.
notes: 2026-07-02 (metis): CLOSED per TOPO gap analysis. Append-only assessment lifecycle is satisfied by ADR-040 (5 TOPO DomainTables registered in SchemaConfig.topo()). The topo_observations, topo_assessments, topo_followups, topo_regimes, and topo_supersedes tables provide immutable facts + append-only assessment revisions + follow-up state machine + regime supersession. TOPO no longer blocked.
close_reason: Closed per TOPO gap-analysis reconciliation 2026-07-02. Append-only assessment lifecycle covered by ADR-040: SchemaConfig.topo() includes topo_observations, topo_assessments, topo_followups, topo_regimes, topo_supersedes DomainTables.

title: TOPO: structured source_refs[] provenance for idempotent event derivation
description: P0 gap from TOPO migration analysis. TOPO's event engine derives stable event_id = sha256(node_id + source + window_start) from CMMS/SAP work orders and stores structured source references. OHM currently has provenance (string) and source_url but no source_refs[] array or deterministic source-signature field. Without this, re-running ingest duplicates events. This blocks TOPO's idempotent event derivation and any domain that needs multi-record provenance (CMMS, SAP, historian).
notes: 2026-07-02 (metis): CLOSED per TOPO gap analysis. Source provenance for CMMS is satisfied by OHM-wdrg (ADR-013 source_url enforcement) + ADR-028 structured source_refs. TOPO can immediately unblock event idempotency using the source_url + source node pattern already in OHM; no new primitive required.
close_reason: Closed per TOPO gap-analysis reconciliation 2026-07-02. CMMS source provenance gap resolved by OHM-wdrg + ADR-028 structured source_refs. TOPO can proceed with source-node + source_url pattern for idempotent event derivation.

title: TOPO: event horizon / plan grouping container (topo_plan equivalent)
description: P0 gap from TOPO migration analysis. TOPO's TopoPlan groups events by purpose (annual outage, PM schedule, campaign) with status, start_ts/end_ts, and rollup queries. OHM has PLANS L4 edge but no first-class plan container. Depends on OHM-ay5k (events). Decision needed: core ohm_plans table vs DomainTable convention.
notes: 2026-07-02 (metis): Re-parented under OHM-dh9l (ADR-041 temporal event model). The plan-container gap is now part of the unified temporal-event epic. Immediate TOPO unblock can proceed via OHM-dh9l.1 DomainTables.

title: TOPO: structured temporal event table architecture (topo_events equivalent)
description: P0 gap from TOPO migration analysis. TOPO's TopoEvent is far richer than ohm_observations: it carries horizon (HISTORICAL/CURRENT/PLANNED/FORECAST), start_ts/end_ts bounded durations, event_class + operating_state (PM, CM, FAILURE, OPERATIONAL...), flow_impact[], l3_context[], forecast_basis, and decision_metadata. OHM observations are point-in-time float values and cannot represent a 4-day planned maintenance window with L2 flow impact and L3 causal context. This issue is the architecture decision for whether OHM gets a core ohm_events table or a DomainTable convention (preferred: core table if events are general across domains, otherwise DomainTable pilot). Blocks TOPO migration and any time-bounded event domain.
notes: 2026-07-02 (metis): Re-parented under OHM-dh9l (ADR-041 temporal event model). The events architecture decision is now part of the unified temporal-event epic. Immediate TOPO unblock can proceed via OHM-dh9l.1 DomainTables.

title: TOPO: Bayesian propagation rewrite via UUID edge traversal and temporal events
description: P1 gap from TOPO migration analysis. TOPO's reliability propagation requires UUID edge traversal and temporal-event-aware Bayesian updates. OHM's existing causal edge types (CAUSES, DEPENDS_ON, ENABLES, INFLUENCES) and Bayesian engine are the right substrate, but need validation with temporal-event inputs once OHM-ay5k (events) lands. This is about wiring the existing OHM Bayesian pipeline to accept event-derived observations, not replacing it.
notes: 2026-07-02 (metis): Re-parented under OHM-dh9l (ADR-041 temporal event model). Temporal-aware Bayesian propagation depends on the unified temporal-event model.

title: TOPO NodeType vocabulary + layer encoding alignment
description:
TOPO's existing store uses UPPERCASE NodeType values and INTEGER layer encoding (1,2,3). OHM uses lowercase types and VARCHAR layer labels ('L1','L2','L3','L4'). Also, TOPO needs node types METRIC, DATA_PRODUCT, COMPONENT, OTHER which are missing from SchemaConfig.topo().

Acceptance:
- SchemaConfig.topo() extended with METRIC, DATA_PRODUCT, COMPONENT, OTHER.
- Migration path documented for converting existing TOPO rows to OHM conventions.
- Optional: SchemaConfig accepts a case_strategy preserving legacy casing per domain.

Not blocking for safe migration, but required before TOPO queries work.
close_reason: Implemented in commit c9b63b0: feat(schema): TOPO NodeType vocab + case_strategy field.

title: Standardize temporal endpoint field names and make mode-switch easier to call
description:
Two API usability issues found during live daemon integration test:

1. POST /temporal/freshness expects decision_id, while the underlying set_freshness_threshold() uses decision_node_id. Accept both or standardize on one.

2. POST /temporal/mode-switch requires from_mode. The underlying recommend_mode() does not. Make from_mode optional by deriving it from the most recent mode_switch node for the decision.

See /root/olympus/metis/memory/2026-06-30_live_daemon_test_report.md
close_reason: Misdiagnosed per dev followup (docs/test-reports/2026-06-30_live_daemon_followup.md). The query function, SDK, and HTTP handler all consistently use decision_id. No mismatch exists.

title: OHM operational twin model selection: statistical models, drift detection, validation, ensemble methods
close_reason: Operational twin model layer landed in commit 1df8304. Tests pass: test_operational_twin_models.py (28 tests).

comments.author: root
comments.text:
Matt asked for a deep dive on model selection, drift detection, validation, parallel models, Karpathy-style improvement, and overfitting avoidance for operational twins.

Design note created: metis:20260630_Operational_Twin_Models_Drift_Validation_Ensemble.md

Key topics covered:
- Statistical models by domain: production plans (PERT, MIP, Monte Carlo), inventory (newsvendor, (R,Q), bootstrap/Croston), capacity (queueing, RCPSP), supply chain (MEIO, robust optimization, discrete-event sim)
- Model selection as multi-criteria decision: predictive accuracy, decision value, interpretability, robustness, latency, cost, overfitting risk
- Parallel model ensemble: active + shadow + candidates, continuous scoring
- Divergence detection: residual drift, feature drift, concept drift, override patterns, ensemble disagreement, constraint proximity
- Validation methods: walk-forward, live shadow, decision replay, stress test, constraint stress test, human review triggers
- Karpathy-style loop: behavioral constraints (what model must not do) + structural constraints (how it must be built)
- Overfitting guards: walk-forward validation, regularization, Bayesian priors, simpler model floor, prediction intervals, fixed retrain schedule
- Model marketplace integration: risk_profile, decision-value metrics, lifecycle gates, audit edges

Related tasks:
- OHM-75tw model marketplace
- OHM-hl61 twin template catalog
- OHM-nbpl supply-chain/production/inventory/capacity templates

My gate_status values (open/closed/stuck) differ from upstream (intact/converted/compromised/failed). Need to decide whether to add decision_gate_status or derive loop readiness from structural status.

5. Still needs adding: validation_policy, override_event, autonomy_level, decay_profile, twin registration, model marketplace, temporal mode switching, /loop endpoint.

6. Twin /predict output can feed directly into /scenario as edge_overrides or node_interventions. Twin /constraints maps to constraint_expr. Twin gate health maps to gate_type/gate_status.

LANDED 2026-06-30 (c52d0ec): internal OHM twin API.

Implemented:

Remaining design gap:
The current contract is internal to OHM. Our design also needs an EXTERNAL snap-in contract where domain twins expose their own endpoints:
- GET /state
- POST /predict
- POST /constraints
- POST /validate-action

OHM should call out to the external twin's endpoint_url for /state and /predict, and combine external twin constraints with OHM's internal graph constraints.

comments.author: root
comments.text:
REFINED SCOPE 2026-06-30:

Per Matt, the snap-in contract must be location-agnostic:
- internal: twin runs inside OHM process using DuckDB / local simulator / ML model
- external: twin runs on separate system; OHM calls endpoint_url
- hybrid: some contract methods external, some internal

Current c52d0ec landed internal OHM API (POST /twin/register, GET /predict, etc.). This is internal mode.

Remaining work:
1. External mode: OHM calls domain twin endpoints at endpoint_url for /state, /predict, /constraints, /validate-action, /explain
2. Hybrid mode: route each method to internal or external based on twin config; merge results
3. Unified executor router with health checks and fallback behavior when external twin is slow/unreachable
4. Twin location decision: internal vs external vs hybrid based on data gravity, compute, latency, security, regulation
5. Add /state endpoint (missing from current implementation)

This closes the gap between landed code and design.

title: OHM model marketplace: compare and evolve models per twin

comments.text: Model outcomes should be stored as observations and scored. Active model selection can influence decision evaluation via DECISION_DEPENDS_ON edges or scenario nodes. Design note 20260630_OHM_Design_to_Code_Reconciliation.md tracks alignment.

comments.author: root
comments.text:
MAJOR DESIGN UPDATE 2026-06-30:

Model marketplace now grounded in DuckDB satellite tables (ohm_twin_models, ohm_twin_predictions, ohm_twin_decisions, ohm_twin_validation_runs).

Model lifecycle: candidate → shadow → active → retired

Model node fields:
- twin_instance_id, target_node_id, target_attribute
- model_family, model_signature {inputs, output, horizon, frequency}
- status, metrics {rmse, mape, decision_value, constraint_violation_rate, robustness_score, latency_ms, costs}
- risk_profile {overfit_risk, drift_sensitivity, tail_risk}
- cost_profile {data_feed_cost, compute_cost, integration_cost}
- promotion_policy {min_shadow_period_days, min_decision_value_improvement, max_constraint_violation_rate}
- weights_ref (external model artifact store for large models)

Scoring function: weighted combination of decision_value, constraint compliance, robustness, minus cost/latency/risk. Domain-tunable weights.

Promotion criteria:
1. Shadow period ≥ threshold
2. Decision value improvement ≥ threshold
3. Constraint violation rate ≤ threshold
4. Robustness competitive
5. Approval required

API surface:
POST /twin/{id}/model — register candidate
GET /twin/{id}/models — list with scores
POST /twin/{id}/model/{model_id}/shadow
POST /twin/{id}/model/{model_id}/promote
POST /twin/{id}/model/{model_id}/retire
GET /twin/{id}/model/{model_id}/predictions
POST /twin/{id}/model/{model_id}/score
GET /twin/{id}/model-marketplace — dashboard

Drift detection SQL examples using ohm_twin_predictions residuals and sliding windows.

Tracked in OHM graph as nodes: decision-value-scoring, model-drift-detection, model-marketplace-schema.
Stored in metis memory/2026-06-30.md during Zettelkasten flush.

comments.author: root
comments.text:
LANDED 2026-06-30 (5967669): core model marketplace primitives.

Implemented:

- COMPETES_WITH and EVALUATED_BY edge types
- Composite score + recommendation
- Promotion marks active and archives others
- SDK and HTTP surfaces

Gaps vs. design (remaining work):
1. Scoring is accuracy-only (mae/rmse/accuracy). Need decision-value scoring: decision_value, constraint_violation_rate, robustness_score, latency, cost, risk.
2. No shadow mode. Lifecycle currently candidate → evaluate → active. Need candidate → shadow → active → retired.
3. No temporal integration: model evaluation should consider data freshness and VoI.
4. No DuckDB satellite tables yet (ohm_twin_models, ohm_twin_predictions, ohm_twin_decisions). Still metadata-in-JSON.
5. No drift detection using prediction residuals.

Core marketplace is landed; decision-value scoring and shadow lifecycle are the next refinement.

2. Add promotion_policy field to model_candidate nodes: {mode: accuracy | decision_value, decision_node_id: ..., min_improvement: float}.
3. Default policy remains accuracy for backward compatibility; explicit decision_value mode uses compute_decision_value.
4. Update /model/{id}/promote endpoint to accept decision_node_id and policy override.

This is P0 for the first external twin pilot (Ledger financial use case).

title: OHM twin construction engine: assemble decision-specific twins from primitives

comments.author: root
comments.text:
Updated 2026-06-30: twin construction is now conversational and iterative, not one-shot. The agent:

1. Starts from a vague user goal
2. Asks clarifying questions (discovery prompts from templates)
3. Passively observes available data and existing OHM nodes
4. Proposes a template and a shaped twin
5. Gets explicit user approval on safety constraints, autonomy level, action alternatives, budget
6. Instantiates, validates, runs baseline scenario
7. Monitors and proposes refinements over time

Key additions:
- POST /twin/design endpoint with session-based stages
- Data discovery pass before twin shape proposal
- Hypothesis-driven refinement after deployment
- User-in-the-loop approval for safety-critical and autonomy properties
- Continuous evolution rules

Updated note: metis:20260630_OHM_Twin_Construction_Engine.md

comments.author: root
comments.text: Design direction selected by Matt 2026-06-30: conversational twin design endpoint /twin/design. Agents interview users, observe data, propose templates, get explicit approval, then deploy and refine. No complete spec required. Detailed design to follow.

comments.author: root
comments.text:
DETAILED DESIGN 2026-06-30: conversational twin design endpoint /twin/design.

Session state machine:
init -> discover -> observe -> propose -> approve -> instantiate -> calibrate -> operate -> evolve

POST /twin/design request:
{ session_id?, user_input, mode (auto/interactive/silent), context { project_id, domain_hints, data_sources, decision_objective, constraints } }

Response includes:
- session_id and stage
- twin_candidate { template, label, domain, node_twins[], edges[], decisions[], models[] }
- questions_for_user[]
- gaps[]
- confidence
- next_action

Key API endpoints:
POST /twin/design/{id}/observe — agent observes data source
POST /twin/design/{id}/bind — bind source to template slot
POST /twin/design/{id}/approve — user approval with provenance
POST /twin/design/{id}/instantiate — deploy twin
POST /twin/design/{id}/evolve — propose upgrade
GET /twin/design/{id}/provenance — audit trail

Agent interview strategy adapts based on what it already knows. Template matching scores domain, decision type, data shape, cadence, constraints, governance. Calibration runs schema validation, historical replay, constraint check, drift baseline, gate health. Evolution loop monitors drift/gate status/user overrides and proposes upgrades.

Stored in metis memory/2026-06-30.md due to Zettelkasten flush. Linked to OHM-josq, OHM-hl61, OHM-75tw, OHM-2x2u.

Open questions:
1. Auto-bind discovered sources or always ask?
2. How to represent partial approvals?
3. Long-lived graph objects vs ephemeral session state?
4. When has agent asked enough vs too much?
5. Multi-agent co-design?

comments.author: root
comments.text:
DOGFOD UPDATE 2026-06-30:

The entire twin framework is now being tracked as OHM nodes in the live graph:
- Pattern node: OHM Decision-Intelligence OS with Snap-In Twins
- 18+ concept nodes including twin, snap-in contract, model marketplace, VSM skeleton, data binding, satellite tables, decision value scoring, model drift detection
- 13+ task/gap nodes including vsm-capture-ui, adapter-schema, streaming-buffer, model-marketplace-schema
- 8+ decision nodes including loop readiness vs structural gate, shared vs per-twin DuckDB, pluggable vs declarative adapters

This is the design graph becoming self-describing inside OHM.

comments.author: root
comments.text:
LANDED 2026-06-30 (5967669): twin construction engine primitives.

Implemented:

Gaps vs. conversational design (remaining work):

3. No data binding discovery/registration step.

comments.author: root
comments.text:
MAJOR DESIGN UPDATE 2026-06-30:

Conversational /twin/design session design. Wraps the landed assemble_twin_for_decision function with a multi-turn agent-user co-design flow.

Session state machine:
init → discover → observe → propose → approve → instantiate (assemble_twin_for_decision) → calibrate → operate → evolve

New table:
ohm_twin_design_sessions (id, user_id, project_id, stage, context JSON, candidate JSON, approved_at, instantiated_twin_id, timestamps)

API surface:
POST /twin/design
GET /twin/design/{id}
POST /twin/design/{id}/answer
POST /twin/design/{id}/observe
POST /twin/design/{id}/bind
POST /twin/design/{id}/approve
POST /twin/design/{id}/instantiate
POST /twin/design/{id}/evolve
GET /twin/design/{id}/provenance

Key conversational features:
- VSM capture via agent questions (processes, inventory, flows, metrics, decisions, data)
- Data binding discovery and approval
- Explicit user approval as action nodes with provenance
- Calibration phase (schema validation, historical replay, constraint backtest, drift baseline, gate health)
- Evolution loop (drift, gate status, overrides, upgrade proposals)

Instantiation step calls existing assemble_twin_for_decision with preferred_template_id and goal, then registers bindings and runs calibration.

Tracked in OHM graph: twin-design-session, vsm-capture, user-approval-provenance, calibration-phase, evolution-loop, assemble-twin-for-decision concepts; conversational-design-implementation gap; session-storage-model decision.

comments.author: root
comments.text:

comments.author: root
comments.text:
NEXT DEV PRIORITY 2026-06-30:

Ledger financial twin pilot needs a way to register/binding feeds, decision, and candidate models without a complete one-shot spec.

Minimum viable flow (before full conversational UX):
1. POST /twin/register — create twin node + bind target decision + list feed bindings + list model candidate IDs.
2. PUT /twin/{id}/bindings — add/remove state feed node bindings.
3. PUT /twin/{id}/models — attach/detach model candidates.
4. GET /twin/{id}/readiness — return which gates are satisfied (feeds present & fresh, models available, decision bound).

This is P0-minimal. Full conversational /twin/design session remains P1.

Design reference: metis zettelkasten notes 20260630_OHM_Twin_Construction_Engine.md and memory/2026-06-30.md section on conversational /twin/design.

comments.author: root
comments.text:
MAJOR DESIGN UPDATE 2026-06-30:

Temporal decision layer designed. Builds on existing OHM primitives:
- decision node type + evaluate_decision()
- ohm_observations (timestamped)
- gate_status columns
- POST /scenario + counterfactual cascade
- propose_action / execute_action / query_loop_status
- twin node + internal contract endpoints
- DuckDB analytical queries

New schema fields on ohm_nodes:
- temporal_mode ('realtime' | 'deliberative' | 'mixed')
- decay_profile (JSON with half_life, min_confidence)
- freshness_requirements (JSON per feed/model)
- urgency_score, decision_horizon, last_evaluated_at, next_evaluation_due

Core components:
1. Freshness service — checks ohm_observations, updates gate_status
2. Mode router — realtime vs deliberative based on urgency + freshness + safety
3. Confidence decay — edge/node confidence degrades with observation age
4. Realtime mode — fast heuristic / active model
5. Deliberative mode — POST /scenario counterfactuals, full evaluation
6. VoI feed investment — decides which data feeds/models are worth paying for
7. POST /decision/{id}/evaluate — unified temporal endpoint
8. /loop-status extended with temporal section

Mode rules:
- urgency > 0.7 + all fresh → realtime
- urgency high but stale → escalate/degraded
- complex tradeoffs / low urgency → deliberative
- safety-critical risk → realtime bypass approval

Minimal code path: schema.py → recommendation.py (evaluate_decision_temporal) → handlers/graph.py (/decision/{id}/evaluate) → tests/test_decision_nodes.py

Tracked in OHM graph: realtime-mode, deliberative-mode, confidence-decay, freshness-service, mode-router concepts + temporal-layer-implementation gap + temporal-schema-location decision.
Stored in metis memory/2026-06-30.md during Zettelkasten flush.

IMPLEMENTATION PATH 2026-06-30:

Concrete dev-ready plan added to /root/olympus/metis/memory/2026-06-30_temporal_layer_impl_path.md.

Implementation sequence:
1. Schema: add temporal columns to ohm_nodes via ALTER TABLE IF NOT EXISTS.
2. New modules: src/ohm/temporal/freshness.py, decay.py, router.py, voi.py.
3. Extend src/ohm/decision/recommendation.py with evaluate_decision_temporal().

Key deliverables:
- Freshness service checks ohm_observations and updates gate_status.
- Mode router selects realtime/deliberative based on urgency + freshness + safety.

This comment closes the design phase. Implementation can begin.

title: OHM agent-readable knowledge surfaces: neighborhood narrative, claim lineage, contradiction summary, task context
description:
OHM stores rich structured knowledge, but agents currently receive low-level node/edge lists and must reconstruct meaning. This epic creates agent-readable surfaces that return contextualized arguments instead of raw graph fragments.

Surfaces to build:
1. Neighborhood narrative — 'You care about X because of Y and Z'
2. Claim lineage — explode a synthesis into supporting observations and sources
3. Contradiction summary — 'These two observations disagree; here's the evidence on each side'
4. Task context — a task bundled with its relevant subgraph and expected outcome
5. Confidence report — which of an agent's beliefs have shifted and why

Why this matters:
- New agents without deep history can become useful immediately.
- Reduces inference latency: agent goes from query to decision faster.
- Makes OHM's Bayesian reasoning legible, not just available.

Acceptance:
- New endpoints or query params return structured narrative+evidence payloads.
- Performance target: <500ms for a typical agent footprint.
- Consumable by both stateful agents (Metis) and ephemeral agents.
- Links to onboarding (/orient, /changes) and task endpoints.

Child issues to create:
- OHM-*.1 neighborhood narrative endpoint
- OHM-*.2 claim lineage endpoint
- OHM-*.3 contradiction summary endpoint
- OHM-*.4 task context binding
- OHM-*.5 per-agent confidence report

title: OHM outcome feedback loop: closing tasks updates beliefs
description:
The task-action bridge (OHM-sdrr) mirrors Beads issues into OHM tasks. This feature closes the loop: when a task is completed, its outcome is recorded as an observation that updates OHM beliefs and source reliability.

Flow:
1. Task assigned to agent via /tasks
2. Agent does work and updates task status to done
3. Task carries expected_claim or expected_observation
4. On close, OHM prompts for outcome (TRUE/FALSE/AMBIGUOUS) and notes
5. Outcome recorded against the claim and the source_agent
6. Bayesian inference updated automatically
7. Source reliability for the agent is recalculated

Why this matters:
- Connects execution back into the belief engine.
- Makes agents accountable for their predictions in a structured way.
- Automates much of the ADR-018 verification workflow.

Acceptance:
- Task schema supports expected_claim and success_criteria fields.
- Closing a task surfaces outcome recording UI/API.
- Outcome auto-links to the original claim via REFERENCES/VERIFIED_BY edge.
- Source reliability updated.
- Contradiction/anomaly checks run after outcome recording.
close_reason: Implemented outcome feedback loop MVP. Schema 0.36.0 adds expected_claim, success_criteria, outcome, outcome_notes columns to ohm_nodes. New query_close_task_with_outcome in queries/__init__.py atomically sets task_status=done, records outcome on the task node, and (when expected_claim or claim_node is set + outcome is TRUE/FALSE) records an ohm_outcomes row via query_record_outcome with source_agent=task.created_by. New HTTP endpoint POST /tasks/{id}/outcome. PATCH /node and PATCH /tasks/{id} now accept expected_claim and success_criteria. SDK (both direct Graph and HTTP client) gains complete_task_with_outcome(). 20/20 new tests in tests/test_task_outcome.py pass; 376/376 schema+queries+server tests pass. Deferred to follow-up: VERIFIED_BY/FALSIFIED_BY edge types, automatic Bayesian reliability cascade, write_node signature extension for expected_claim.

title: Fix /orient endpoint 500 error: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable
description:
/orient?agent=<agent> returns HTTP 500 with {'error': 'internal_error', 'message': "'NoneType' object is not subscriptable"}. This breaks the context-recovery flow documented in AGENTS.md and OHM Writing Protocol.

Reproduction:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" 'http://127.0.0.1:8710/orient?agent=metis'

Expected:
Returns orientation payload with where_was_i, what_did_i_miss, what_next.

Actual:
500 internal_error, correlation_id fb97ac75-83f8-4db2-b821-d5b1391bed55.

Impact:
- Every agent session must manually reconstruct context from daily notes.
- /orient is a primary onboarding/recovery endpoint.

Acceptance:
- /orient?agent=<valid_agent> returns valid JSON for all registered agents.
- /orient handles missing/unknown agents gracefully (empty result, not 500).
- Correlation_id present in logs.
- Regression test added to HTTP integration suite.
close_reason: Hardened /orient endpoint against 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable 500s. Defensive fix in src/ohm/server/handlers/analysis.py:1061 — _get_orient now wraps fetchone() in a _scalar helper (returns None when fetchone() returns None), guards the sparse_nodes r[4]<=1 filter against NULL edge_count, defends island iteration against dict/None, and uses a _coerce_iso helper for datetime/None/str coercion. Added 4 regression tests in tests/test_server.py::TestOrientEndpoint covering: unknown agent (cold_start), missing agent param (400), seeded activity (full packet), empty DB (no fetchone crash). All 4 new tests pass; 91 of 91 non-hook server tests pass (6 hook test failures pre-exist on main).

title: Agent-facing Beads-OHM sync: mirror assigned issues as OHM tasks
description:
Complement the task-routing bug fix with a designed integration: Beads is the canonical issue store, OHM task nodes are the runtime representation. Build a sync layer that keeps them aligned.

Design questions:
- Which system is source of truth? (Beads for planning, OHM for runtime state)
- How often does sync run? (heartbeat, webhook, or cron)
- What fields map? (Beads id -> OHM task.external_ref, assignee -> assigned_to, priority, status, labels -> tags)
- How do closed/duplicate Beads issues clean up OHM tasks?

Acceptance:
- New Beads issue with assignee creates OHM task within one sync cycle.
- Status changes in either system propagate to the other (with conflict rule).
- Agent can claim/complete work through OHM API and Beads reflects it.
- Document the mapping and source-of-truth rules.
close_reason: Implemented Beads → OHM task sync MVP. New module src/ohm/integrations/beads_sync.py with sync_beads_to_ohm_tasks() — mirrors assigned Beads issues into OHM task nodes. Field mapping: bd.id→metadata.beads_id, title→label, description→content, status→task_status (via BD_STATUS_MAP), priority 0-4→P0-P3, assignee→assigned_to (strips @olympus.local), labels→tags. Creates a beads-backlog-anchor concept node for cross-link enforcement (ADR-018). Status precedence prevents OHM-side status regressions (done stays done even if Beads reopens). Idempotent: re-sync updates existing tasks. New HTTP endpoint POST /admin/sync-beads accepts explicit issues list or fetches via bd CLI / JSONL fallback. 20/20 tests pass in tests/test_beads_sync.py (3 assignee normalization, 13 direct query, 4 HTTP integration). 285/285 regression tests pass. Deferred to follow-up: OHM→Beads bidirectional sync, webhook-triggered real-time sync, closed/duplicate cleanup, CLI command wrapper.

title: Fix residual DuckDB concurrency segfaults in suggestion/nudge threads
description: HTTP integration tests intermittently segfault when the background suggestion thread runs semantic_search/Ollama embedding while the main request handler thread writes to the same DuckDB store. Earlier fix (5a1d796) reduced occurrences but full-suite runs still crash. Need to serialize or isolate background suggestion DB access.
close_reason: Sync drift cleanup: OHM-k0bi was closed in JSONL (committed 589b214) but the local Dolt DB still showed it open/in_progress. Code fix landed in 305230a (synchronous post-write suggestions under request write lock with 0.5s deadline budget, ThreadPoolExecutor removed). Full default pytest suite verified passing (2567 passed, 6 skipped, 109 deselected) at close time. Re-import of JSONL did not reconcile status, so closing manually to restore canonical state from the git-tracked export.

comments.author: root
comments.text:
Root cause: post-write suggestions in _post_node were launched in a per-request ThreadPoolExecutor worker that opened a fresh read-only DuckDB connection but still raced with the main request handler thread and with concurrent writes during full-suite runs.

Fix in 305230a:
- Removed the ThreadPoolExecutor; suggestions now run synchronously on the request thread under the existing write lock.
- Added a configurable deadline budget (SUGGESTION_TIMEOUT_S = 0.5s) passed through generate_suggestions, generate_edge_suggestions, generate_connectivity_nudge, and generate_island_nudge.
- Deadline-aware helpers skip expensive work (semantic search / island detection) when time is exhausted.
- Ollama embedding timeout is propagated from the remaining budget so a slow/down Ollama doesn't blow the response time.
- _post_scratch and _post_edge use the same budget.

Test results:
- tests/test_suggestions.py: 26/26 pass (previously 2 integration tests were timing out and dropping suggestions).
- Combined bedrock/document/suggestion/s3 suite: 70/70 pass.

Remaining risk: other background threads (semantic metric actions, ducklake sync, fragment eviction) still run concurrently. This fix targets only the post-write suggestion/nudge thread path. Monitor full-suite runs under OHM-zaow.
