title: Refactor markov.py to accept GraphReader
description: Part of OHM-p2u8. Change markov_absorbing_risk() and markov_expected_steps() to accept GraphReader instead of conn. Smaller change than bayesian.py — Markov functions have simpler graph queries.
close_reason: Implemented in prior session: SemanticRoles dataclass, DuckDBGraphReader, bayesian/markov refactors all landed and passing tests.

title: Add MockGraphReader and convert inference unit tests to not require database
description: Part of OHM-p2u8. Implement MockGraphReader that accepts in-memory lists of records. Convert existing Bayesian and Markov tests that currently spin up a full server+DuckDB to use MockGraphReader with fixture data instead. Tests should be faster and more focused — test the inference math, not the DB integration.
close_reason: Implemented: SemanticRoles wired into build_bayesian_network/compute_voi/compute_ate/markov functions. MockGraphReader tests in test_graph_reader.py.

title: Define GraphReader Protocol and Edge/Node/Observation record types
description: Foundation for OHM-p2u8. Define the Protocol class and the record dataclasses (EdgeRecord, NodeRecord, ObservationRecord) that form the contract between inference and storage. Keep the protocol minimal — only the queries inference actually makes, not a full graph query API. Edge/Node/Observation records should be frozen dataclasses, not raw tuples, so callers have named fields.
close_reason: Already implemented in the restructure/GraphReader/SemanticRoles/IngestAdapter work from this session.

title: Add SemanticRoles config to make inference engine edge-type-agnostic
description:
OHM's inference engine hardcodes its own edge type names throughout: compute_voi hardcodes ['CAUSES','INFLUENCES','ENABLES','DEPENDS_ON'], markov hardcodes ['TRANSITIONS_TO'], build_bayesian_network hardcodes ['CAUSES','DEPENDS_ON','THREATENS','EXPECTED_LIKELIHOOD','NEGATES']. Domain apps with different vocabulary — TOPO uses DEGRADES_TO, FAILED_BY, ESCALATES_TO — must either adopt OHM's names or pass edge_types on every single inference call. This couples the inference engine to OHM's specific vocabulary, making it unworkable as a generic framework.

What: SemanticRoles is a configuration dataclass mapping abstract inference roles to domain-specific edge type lists. Inference functions accept optional SemanticRoles and use role-based lookups instead of hardcoded strings. SchemaConfig grows a semantic_roles field with OHM defaults so existing behavior is unchanged.

Design:
@dataclass
class SemanticRoles:
    causal: list[str] = ['CAUSES','INFLUENCES','ENABLES','DEPENDS_ON']
    state_transitions: list[str] = ['TRANSITIONS_TO']
    negating: list[str] = ['NEGATES']
    evidential: list[str] = ['SUPPORTS','CHALLENGED_BY']
    bayesian: list[str] = ['CAUSES','DEPENDS_ON','THREATENS','EXPECTED_LIKELIHOOD','NEGATES']
acceptance_criteria: OHM default behavior unchanged when no SemanticRoles passed (defaults match current hardcoded values). TOPO can pass SemanticRoles(causal=['DEGRADES_TO','CAUSES_FAILURE']) and get correct VoI rankings. No hardcoded edge type lists remain in compute_voi, markov_absorbing_risk, or build_bayesian_network. Depends on OHM-p2u8 (GraphReader) being clean first.
close_reason: Already implemented in the restructure/GraphReader/SemanticRoles/IngestAdapter work from this session.

title: Define GraphReader protocol to decouple inference engine from DuckDB
description:
bayesian.py, markov.py, and other inference modules accept a raw DuckDB conn and run SQL directly against ohm_nodes/ohm_edges/ohm_observations. This has three consequences: (1) inference functions are unusable as a library — domain apps like TOPO cannot call compute_voi() without a live daemon connection; (2) inference is untestable without a real database — current tests spin up a full server just to test a Bayesian computation; (3) no alternative storage backend is possible without rewriting inference code.

What: GraphReader is a Python Protocol defining the graph access surface inference functions need. DuckDBGraphReader implements it against the current schema. Inference functions accept GraphReader instead of conn. Tests inject MockGraphReader with in-memory fixture data.

Design:
class GraphReader(Protocol):
    def get_edges(self, *, edge_types, layers=None) -> list[EdgeRecord]: ...
    def get_nodes(self, ids=None, *, node_type=None) -> list[NodeRecord]: ...
    def get_observations(self, node_id: str) -> list[ObservationRecord]: ...
    def get_meta(self, key: str) -> str | None: ...
    def get_graph_generation(self) -> int: ...
acceptance_criteria: compute_voi(), compute_ate(), markov_absorbing_risk(), build_bayesian_network() all accept GraphReader not conn. DuckDBGraphReader passes existing integration tests. MockGraphReader enables unit tests without database. TOPO can import and call inference functions with its own GraphReader implementation.
close_reason: Already implemented: GraphReader protocol (OHM-n95j), DuckDBGraphReader (OHM-1s1k), MockGraphReader tests (OHM-ljef), bayesian/markov refactors (OHM-l1y0, OHM-s5so), SemanticRoles (OHM-f2av) all shipped.

title: Challenge edge metadata display fields are null in API responses
description: Challenge edges are accepted and appear in audit log, but the edge metadata display fields (likely challenge_of, challenge_type, or probability percentile fields) come back null in API responses. The data is written correctly but may not be selected/returned by the read queries. Likely cosmetic — the underlying data is correct.
close_reason: Closed

title: Heartbeat crashes with 500: ohm_change_feed table does not exist
description:
## Bug: Heartbeat endpoint crashes because ohm_change_feed missing from production DB

### Description
From Deepthought's stress test + confirmed with live daemon:

```
POST /heartbeat → 500: Catalog Error: Table with name ohm_change_feed does not exist!
LINE 3:                 FROM ohm_change_feed
```

### Root cause
`agent_heartbeat()` in `methods.py` calls `_log_change()` which inserts into `ohm_change_feed`. The schema defines this table in `schema.py` line 416-426, but the production DB at `/var/lib/ohm/ohm.duckdb` doesn't have it — likely because it was initialized before the change_feed migration, or the migration path doesn't cover this table.

### Impact
- Per-agent local DuckDB writes that sync via DuckLake never reach the HTTP API because the daemon's heartbeat sync path is broken
- Local writes → sync_heartbeat (pushes to DuckLake) → daemon tries to consume → crashes
- All agents using the per-agent OhmStore for zero-latency writes lose sync with the shared graph

### Fix
Add a migration that creates `ohm_change_feed` if it doesn't exist. The table schema is:

```sql
CREATE SEQUENCE IF NOT EXISTS seq_change_feed START 1;
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ohm_change_feed (
    id          BIGINT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT nextval('seq_change_feed'),
    table_name  VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    row_id      VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    operation   VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    agent_name  VARCHAR NOT NULL,
    old_data    JSON,
    new_data    JSON,
    occurred_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

Also create indexes:
```sql
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_feed_agent ON ohm_change_feed(agent_name);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_feed_time ON ohm_change_feed(occurred_at);
```

### Priority
P1 — blocks the per-agent local DB bridge (ADR-012)
close_reason: Closed

title: Consider log/sigmoid scaling for PERT variance instead of linear ×36
description:
## Enhancement: PERT variance scaling may compress meaningful differences

### Description
From Socrates code review:

Current scaling:
```python
pert_variance = ((p95 - p05) / 6.0) ** 2
scaled_variance = min(1.0, pert_variance * 36.0)
```

The ×36 linear scaling maps PERT variance range [0, ~0.028] to [0, ~1.0]. This treats the difference between σ²=0.01 and σ²=0.02 the same as σ²=0.5 and σ²=1.0. But in VoI, what matters is *relative* uncertainty — the difference between 'fairly sure' (σ²=0.01) and 'guessing' (σ²=0.02) is more decision-relevant than the difference between two high-uncertainty estimates.

### Recommendation
Consider log or sigmoid scaling:
```python
scaled_variance = 1.0 - math.exp(-pert_variance * 100)  # exponential decay
```

This preserves ordinal ranking while compressing the high end. The exact parameters need tuning, but linear scaling treats all variance as equally informative when it's not.

### Priority
P3 — tuning, not a correctness bug. Current scaling is functional but suboptimal.

title: Populate utility_scale on decision nodes for VoI prioritization
description:
## Enhancement: Decision nodes need utility_scale for meaningful VoI

### Description
From Socrates code review:

VoI weights sensitivity by utility_scale:
```python
sensitivity += abs(ate_value) * (info.get('utility_scale') or 0.5)
```

But the three existing decision nodes probably don't have utility_scale set, so everything defaults to 0.5. This means VoI can't distinguish between 'being wrong about agent governance matters a LOT' vs. 'being wrong about this other thing matters somewhat' — they all get the same weight.

### Action items
Populate utility_scale on decision nodes:
- decision-agent-governance-standard: utility_scale=0.9 (high-stakes: security, economic impact)
- decision-hormuz-response: utility_scale=0.8 (high-stakes: geopolitical, economic impact)
- ariana_college_decision_2026: utility_scale=0.3 (personal stakes, lower global impact)

### Priority
P3 — content work, not a code change

title: Add min_observations threshold for VoI reliability
description:
## Enhancement: Flag VoI rankings based on insufficient data

### Description
From Socrates code review:

The VoI endpoint returns nodes with 0-1 observations as high-priority research targets. With 1 observation, 'uncertainty' is essentially (1 - confidence) = 0.15, which is not meaningful statistical uncertainty — it's just the inverse of a single point estimate.

### Recommendation
Add a `min_observations` parameter to `compute_voi` (default: 3). Nodes below the threshold should still appear in rankings but flagged as 'insufficient data — high VoI estimate is unreliable.' This prevents the system from confidently recommending research targets based on single observations.

### Priority
P2 — quality improvement for production use
close_reason: Closed

title: VoI sensitivity methods (ATE vs path_confidence) produce non-comparable rankings
description:
## Design: Mixed sensitivity methods make VoI rankings non-comparable

### Description
From Socrates code review:

The /voi output shows two sensitivity methods: `path_confidence` and `ate`. Governance nodes use path_confidence (product of edge confidences, ~0.3-0.5 range). Hormuz nodes use ATE (causal effect size, ~0.02-0.12 range). These are fundamentally different scales:

- **Path confidence**: 'how confident are we in the path' (0-1, typically 0.3-0.5)
- **ATE**: 'how much does changing this node change the outcome' (0-1, typically 0.02-0.12)

Noble Lie (path_confidence=0.425) and Hormuz AND-Gate (ATE=0.373) look comparable but measure different things.

### Recommendation
Standardize on one method. ATE is better (model-based, accounts for confounders). Path_confidence should be explicit fallback only when ATE times out. Output should clearly label which method each node uses so users don't compare apples to oranges.

### Priority
P2 — design improvement, not a correctness bug

title: BFS path traversal misses alternative paths in VoI variance
description:
## Bug: _max_edge_pert_variance_toward skips visited nodes before exploring all paths

### Description
From Socrates code review:

The BFS in `_max_edge_pert_variance_toward` uses a `visited` set to avoid revisiting nodes. This means it only finds ONE path per source→target pair. For DAGs with multiple paths (diamond patterns), the 'max variance across all paths' is actually 'max variance across the first path found for each reachable target.'

If two paths share an intermediate node, only the first path is explored. This can underestimate the max variance and produce incorrect VoI rankings.

### Fix
For DAGs (which Bayesian networks are), remove the early visited-marking and use proper path enumeration. Mark nodes as visited only after all paths through them have been explored, or use a proper DAG path enumeration algorithm.

### Priority
P2 — affects correctness for diamond-shaped causal graphs, but the current implementation is conservative (underestimates variance), not aggressive

title: PERT fallback defaults create degenerate variance estimates
description:
## Bug: Missing p05/p95 silently produce fixed-spread PERT triples

### Description
From Socrates code review:

When `prob_p50` is provided but `prob_p05` or `prob_p95` are missing, the code defaults:
```python
p05 = float(prob_p05) if prob_p05 is not None else float(prob_p50) * 0.8
p95 = float(prob_p95) if prob_p95 is not None else min(1.0, float(prob_p50) * 1.2)
```

This creates a fixed ±20% spread regardless of actual uncertainty. For p50=0.7: p05=0.56, p95=0.84, variance = ((0.84-0.56)/6)² = 0.00218 — always the same.

An expert who only gives p50=0.7 without bounds is MORE uncertain than one who gives p05=0.65, p50=0.7, p95=0.75, but the code makes them look equally certain. This is backwards.

### Fix
Two options:
- **(a) Don't compute PERT variance without full triples.** Fall back to confidence-based uncertainty (honest about what we don't know).
- **(b) Use wider defaults that encode more uncertainty:** `p05 = p50 * 0.5`, `p95 = min(1.0, p50 * 1.5)`. Makes the spread proportional to p50, not fixed at ±20%.

Option (a) is cleaner — if we don't have the PERT triple, we shouldn't pretend we do.

### Priority
P2 — affects VoI uncertainty estimation for edges with only p50

title: VoI double-counts confidence penalty for non-PERT nodes (bug)
description:
## Bug: gap_score squares (1-confidence) for nodes without PERT data

### Description
From Socrates code review:

In `generate_voi_tasks`, the gap_score formula is:
```
gap_score = uncertainty * sensitivity * (1.0 - confidence)
```

But for nodes without PERT data, `uncertainty` is already derived from `(1 - confidence)` as a fallback. So:
```
gap_score = (1 - confidence) * sensitivity * (1 - confidence)
          = sensitivity * (1 - confidence)²
```

This squares the confidence penalty for non-PERT nodes, making them artificially high-VoI compared to PERT nodes. A node with confidence=0.5 gets penalized (0.5)²=0.25, while a PERT node with the same confidence but variance-derived uncertainty might get penalized only 0.15 × confidence × sensitivity.

### Fix
When PERT data exists, use PERT variance as uncertainty. When it doesn't, use (1-confidence). Never multiply (1-confidence) into gap_score again — it's already in the uncertainty component.

The fix should be in `compute_voi` and/or `generate_voi_tasks`: ensure uncertainty is computed once (from PERT variance OR from (1-confidence), never both), and gap_score = uncertainty × sensitivity (no additional confidence term).

### Priority
P1 — directly affects VoI ranking accuracy and task assignment quality

title: PATCH /edge doesn't invalidate Bayesian network cache
description:
## Bug: Stale Bayesian network cache after PERT updates

### Symptom
ATE, sensitivity, and refute endpoints return `Target not in Bayesian network` for nodes added after the Bayesian network cache was first built. The error message reports "network has 10 nodes" even when the graph has 50+ nodes.

Restarting ohmd temporarily fixes the issue (cache clears on restart), but the next cache build goes stale again on any PATCH /edge.

### Root Cause
The PATCH /edge handler updates PERT fields and probability in the database, then calls `self.store._log_change()`, but does NOT call `self.store._increment_graph_generation()`.

The Bayesian network cache checks `ohm_meta.graph_generation` to decide if the cached network is stale. Since PATCH doesn't increment it, the cache returns stale data.

### Fix (already pushed as 68baab8)
Added `self.store._increment_graph_generation()` after `self.store._log_change()` in the PATCH /edge handler.

### Reproduction
1. Start ohmd, add nodes/edges via POST
2. PATCH edges with PERT values
3. /ate returns "Target not in Bayesian network" for recently-added nodes
4. Restart ohmd — ATE works again

### Impact
All causal inference endpoints (ATE, sensitivity, refute, inference, intervene) affected when PERT values updated via PATCH.

title: DuckLake sync interval 60s means up to 60s of data loss on hard shutdown
description: DuckLake sync runs every 60 seconds (config sync_interval_seconds). If ohmd is killed between syncs, all writes since the last sync are lost. With CHECKPOINT on SIGTERM this is mitigated for graceful shutdown, but SIGKILL/OOM still loses up to 60s of data. Options: (1) Reduce sync interval to 10-15s, (2) Sync after every N writes (e.g., every 10 mutations), (3) Sync immediately after decision-node or high-value edge creation.
close_reason: Fixed: background daemon thread in run_server() syncs DuckLake every sync_interval_seconds (default 60s); shutdown handler also triggers a final sync before CHECKPOINT

title: /voi endpoint performance: ATE computation is O(n×d) for large graphs — needs timeout or graph size limit
description: compute_voi() calls compute_ate() for each (candidate, decision) pair. ATE builds a Bayesian network + runs do-operator for each pair. For a graph with 50+ nodes and 5+ decision nodes, this could take 10+ seconds. The /voi HTTP endpoint has no timeout. Fix: (1) Add top_k limit to ATE computation (only compute ATE for top-N candidates by path_confidence), (2) Add timeout parameter, (3) Cache ATE results within a single /voi call.
close_reason: Fixed: compute_voi() now accepts timeout parameter; ATE is skipped for remaining candidates when deadline is exceeded, falling back to path-confidence. Server passes ?timeout= query param.

title: Markov HTTP endpoints
description: Markov chain analysis (markov_absorbing_risk, markov_expected_steps) only works via direct DB access (SDK with :memory: or connect()). No HTTP endpoints expose these functions. Since ohmd holds the DuckDB lock, agents can't use Markov analysis through the daemon. Add GET /markov/absorbing_risk?start=node_id and GET /markov/expected_steps?start=node_id endpoints.

title: Markov Chains: add /markov/absorbing and /markov/expected_steps HTTP endpoints
description: The markov.py module works with direct DB connections, but can't be used through the HTTP API because DuckDB only allows one connection. Need HTTP endpoints that route through ohmd's connection: GET /markov/absorbing?start=recon&edge_types=TRANSITIONS_TO and GET /markov/expected_steps?start=recon&target=exfil
close_reason: Fixed: added GET /markov/absorbing and GET /markov/expected_steps endpoints routing through ohmd's DuckDB connection

title: Test suite: consolidate shared helpers and fixtures into conftest.py
description:
Several shared helpers and fixtures are duplicated across test files:

1. _run_cli helper: Identical 16-line function in test_cli_integration.py (line 12) and test_topo_cli.py (line 95). Move to conftest.py.

2. Bayesian db fixture: Identical fixture in 4 files (test_bayesian.py, test_bayesian_context.py, test_bayesian_sensitivity.py, test_bayesian_adr.py) — all just return create_test_db(). Move to conftest.py.

3. Bayesian causal_graph/causal_chain: test_bayesian.py and test_bayesian_context.py have functionally identical fixtures. Merge into one shared fixture.

4. Server helpers: test_server.py and test_concurrent.py have similar but not identical _start_server and _request helpers. Extract a shared base with extensions for concurrent-specific retry logic.

5. test_soft_delete.py: Uses file-based temp DB (tempfile.mkstemp + WAL cleanup) when OhmStore supports :memory:. Simplify to OhmStore(db_path=':memory:').

6. test_voi_validation.py: 5 instances of duckdb.connect(':memory:'); initialize_schema(conn) instead of create_test_db(). Replace with helper call.

Group by validator function: valid_identifiers, invalid_identifiers, valid_layers, invalid_layers, valid_timestamps, invalid_timestamps, valid_confidences, invalid_confidences, valid_depths, invalid_depths, valid_pert_triples, invalid_pert_triples. Estimated reduction: ~120 lines, same coverage.

title: Large module decomposition: sdk.py (3309 lines), bayesian.py (2768 lines)
description:
sdk.py (3309 lines) and bayesian.py (2768 lines) are very large single modules. Consider decomposition:

sdk.py: Split domain-specific method groups (customer support, cybersecurity, medical, VoI) into mixins or separate submodules. The Graph class has 50+ methods covering discovery, write, read, analysis, collaboration, change feed, substrate, export/import, and multiple domain workflows.

Also notable: store.py (2303 lines), queries/__init__.py (2813 lines), cli/__init__.py (1309 lines), server.py (1102 lines). These are large but follow a more uniform pattern.

Should add a [bayesian] or [science] optional dependency group so users can pip install ohm[bayesian] to get these packages. Also pgmpy if needed.

If 3.10 is actually supported, update AGENTS.md. Also update ruff target-version (currently py310) and mypy python_version (currently 3.10) if moving to 3.12+.

This works at runtime but is unusual and causes mypy complaints. Should be refactored to a proper @classmethod on the SchemaConfig class, like the existing topo() classmethod.

The queries/ module correctly uses parameterized queries with question-mark placeholders for the same pattern (see query_neighborhood lines 106-110). Should be refactored to use parameterized queries.
